intro_text: Hi there! I'm Rishika, and today we explore the geometry of lines! 📐 Railway tracks, zebra crossings, window grills — parallel and intersecting lines are all around us!
complete_message: Brilliant work! You've mastered Parallel and Intersecting Lines! ⭐

explain_questions.question: What are parallel lines? Give a real-life example.
explain_questions.question_spoken: What are parallel lines? Give a real-life example.

steps.t: Parallel lines: two lines that never meet, always same distance apart.
steps.s: Parallel lines are two lines that never intersect no matter how far they are extended. They are always the same distance apart.

steps.t: Symbol for parallel: AB || CD means line AB is parallel to line CD.
steps.s: We use two vertical bars to show parallel lines. AB double bar CD means line AB is parallel to line CD.

steps.t: Real-life examples: railway tracks, opposite sides of a ruler, window grills.
steps.s: Real-life examples include railway tracks, the opposite sides of a ruler, and the bars of a window grill.

nudges: Think about railway tracks. Do they ever cross each other?
Parallel lines maintain the same distance throughout.
No! Parallel lines never meet, no matter how far extended!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q1s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Parallel lines: never meet, always same distance</text></g><g id="q1s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Symbol: AB || CD</text></g><g id="q1s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Examples: railway tracks, ruler edges</text></g><g id="q1ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">No, parallel lines never meet</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What is a transversal?

steps.t: A transversal is a line that crosses two or more lines at different points.
steps.s: A transversal is a line that intersects two or more other lines at distinct points.

steps.t: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, 8 angles are formed.
steps.s: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, it creates 8 angles in total — 4 at each intersection.

steps.t: These angles have special relationships: equal or supplementary.
steps.s: These 8 angles have special relationships with each other. Some are equal and some are supplementary.

explain_questions.confirm_question: How many angles are formed when a transversal cuts two parallel lines?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: How many angles are formed when a transversal cuts two parallel lines?
nudges: Count angles at each intersection point.
There are 4 angles at the first intersection and 4 at the second.
4 + 4 = 8 angles total!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q2s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Transversal: crosses 2+ lines at different points</text></g><g id="q2s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Cuts 2 parallel lines → 8 angles formed</text></g><g id="q2s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Angles are equal or supplementary</text></g><g id="q2ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">8 angles</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What are corresponding angles? Are they equal when lines are parallel?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What are corresponding angles? Are they equal when lines are parallel?

steps.t: Corresponding angles are on the same side of the transversal, one above and one below.
steps.s: Corresponding angles are in matching positions at each intersection — both above the parallel line or both below, on the same side of the transversal.

steps.t: They are like a copy of each other at different intersections. F-shape.
steps.s: They look like an F-shape. One angle is at the top intersection and its corresponding angle is at the bottom intersection in the same position.

steps.t: When lines are parallel, corresponding angles are EQUAL.
steps.s: When the two lines are parallel, corresponding angles are always equal to each other.

explain_questions.confirm_question: If lines are parallel and one corresponding angle is 65 degrees, what is the other?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: If lines are parallel and one corresponding angle is 65 degrees, what is the other corresponding angle?
nudges: Corresponding angles have a special relationship when lines are parallel.
They are equal when lines are parallel.
The other corresponding angle is also 65 degrees!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q3s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Corresponding angles: same position at each crossing</text></g><g id="q3s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Look like F-shape</text></g><g id="q3s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Parallel lines → corresponding angles EQUAL</text></g><g id="q3ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">65 degrees (equal)</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What are alternate interior angles?

steps.t: Interior angles are between the two parallel lines.
steps.s: Interior angles are the angles formed between the two parallel lines, not outside them.

steps.t: Alternate interior angles are on OPPOSITE sides of the transversal.
steps.s: Alternate interior angles are on opposite sides of the transversal. One is on the left, the other on the right.

steps.t: When lines are parallel, alternate interior angles are EQUAL. Z-shape.
steps.s: When lines are parallel, alternate interior angles are equal. They form a Z-shape or N-shape.

explain_questions.confirm_question: Alternate interior angles form which letter shape?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Alternate interior angles form which letter shape?
nudges: Think of the shape formed by the two parallel lines and the transversal.
The angles are inside the parallel lines on opposite sides.
They form a Z-shape (or N-shape)!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q4s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Interior angles: between the parallel lines</text></g><g id="q4s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Alternate = on OPPOSITE sides of transversal</text></g><g id="q4s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Parallel lines → alternate interior angles EQUAL (Z)</text></g><g id="q4ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Z-shape (equal)</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What are co-interior angles and what is their sum?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What are co-interior angles and what is their sum when lines are parallel?

steps.t: Co-interior angles are both between the parallel lines, on the SAME side of transversal.
steps.s: Co-interior angles are also called same-side interior angles. They are both between the parallel lines and on the same side of the transversal.

steps.t: They form a C-shape or U-shape.
steps.s: They form a C-shape or U-shape between the two parallel lines.

steps.t: When lines are parallel, co-interior angles add up to 180 degrees (supplementary).
steps.s: When lines are parallel, co-interior angles are supplementary — they always add up to 180 degrees.

explain_questions.confirm_question: If one co-interior angle is 70 degrees, what is the other?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: If one co-interior angle is 70 degrees, what is the other co-interior angle?
nudges: Co-interior angles are supplementary. They add up to 180 degrees.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q5s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Co-interior: same side of transversal, between lines</text></g><g id="q5s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Form C-shape or U-shape</text></g><g id="q5s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Parallel lines → co-interior angles sum = 180°</text></g><g id="q5ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">110 degrees</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What are vertically opposite angles and what is their relationship?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What are vertically opposite angles and what is their relationship?

steps.t: Vertically opposite angles are formed when two lines intersect at a point.
steps.s: When two lines cross each other, they form 4 angles. The angles opposite each other are called vertically opposite angles.

steps.t: They are across the intersection point from each other. X-shape.
steps.s: They are diagonally opposite at the intersection, forming an X-shape.

steps.t: Vertically opposite angles are always EQUAL.
steps.s: Vertically opposite angles are always equal to each other, regardless of whether lines are parallel.

explain_questions.confirm_question: Two lines intersect. One angle is 55 degrees. What is its vertically opposite angle?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Two lines intersect. One angle is 55 degrees. What is its vertically opposite angle?
nudges: Vertically opposite angles have a special property.
They are always equal.
The vertically opposite angle is also 55 degrees!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q6s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Two lines cross → 4 angles formed</text></g><g id="q6s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Opposite angles = vertically opposite (X-shape)</text></g><g id="q6s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Vertically opposite angles are always EQUAL</text></g><g id="q6ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">55 degrees</text></g>

explain_questions.question: What is a linear pair of angles?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is a linear pair of angles?

steps.t: A linear pair: two adjacent angles that form a straight line together.
steps.s: A linear pair is formed when two adjacent angles are together on a straight line.

steps.t: They share a common vertex and a common arm.
steps.s: The two angles share a common vertex (corner point) and one common arm (ray).

steps.t: Linear pair angles are supplementary: they add up to 180 degrees.
steps.s: Because they form a straight line together, linear pair angles always add up to 180 degrees.

explain_questions.confirm_question: One angle of a linear pair is 120 degrees. Find the other.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: One angle of a linear pair is 120 degrees. Find the other angle.
nudges: Linear pair angles add up to 180 degrees.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q7s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Linear pair: 2 adjacent angles on a straight line</text></g><g id="q7s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Share common vertex and common arm</text></g><g id="q7s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Linear pair sum = 180° (supplementary)</text></g><g id="q7ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">60 degrees</text></g>

explain_questions.question: AB || CD. A transversal cuts them. Corresponding angle at AB is 75 degrees. Find the corresponding angle at CD.
explain_questions.question_spoken: AB is parallel to CD. A transversal cuts them. The corresponding angle at AB is 75 degrees. Find the corresponding angle at CD.

steps.t: AB || CD means the lines are parallel.
steps.s: AB double bar CD means line AB is parallel to line CD.

steps.t: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.
steps.s: Since the lines are parallel, corresponding angles are equal.

steps.t: Corresponding angle at CD = 75 degrees.
steps.s: So the corresponding angle at CD is also 75 degrees.

explain_questions.confirm_question: If AB || CD and a co-interior angle at AB is 80 degrees, find the co-interior angle at CD.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: If AB is parallel to CD and a co-interior angle at AB is 80 degrees, find the co-interior angle at CD.
nudges: Co-interior angles are supplementary when lines are parallel.
They add up to 180 degrees.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q8s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">AB || CD (parallel lines)</text></g><g id="q8s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Corresponding angles equal when parallel</text></g><g id="q8s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Angle at CD = 75°</text></g><g id="q8ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">75 degrees</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Two lines are cut by a transversal. Alternate interior angles are 60 and 60 degrees. Are the lines parallel?
explain_questions.question_spoken: Two lines are cut by a transversal. The alternate interior angles are 60 and 60 degrees. Are the lines parallel?

steps.t: If alternate interior angles are equal, the lines ARE parallel.
steps.s: The converse is also true: if alternate interior angles are equal, then the lines must be parallel.

steps.t: Here: both alternate interior angles = 60 degrees. They are equal.
steps.s: Both alternate interior angles are 60 degrees, so they are equal.

steps.t: Yes! Equal alternate interior angles prove the lines are parallel.
steps.s: Since the alternate interior angles are equal, we can conclude the lines are parallel.

explain_questions.confirm_question: If co-interior angles are 95 and 85 degrees, are the lines parallel?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: If co-interior angles are 95 and 85 degrees, are the lines parallel?
nudges: Check if co-interior angles add up to 180 degrees.
95 + 85 = 180 degrees. Yes, the lines are parallel!
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q9s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Equal alternate interior angles → lines parallel</text></g><g id="q9s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Both = 60° → equal!</text></g><g id="q9s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Yes, the lines ARE parallel!</text></g><g id="q9ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Yes, lines are parallel</text></g>

explain_questions.question: AB || CD. Transversal PQ makes angle APQ = 3x + 10 and angle CQP = 2x + 30 (co-interior). Find x.
explain_questions.question_spoken: AB is parallel to CD. A transversal PQ makes angle APQ equal to 3x plus 10 degrees and angle CQP equal to 2x plus 30 degrees. These are co-interior angles. Find x.

steps.t: Co-interior angles sum to 180 degrees when lines are parallel.
steps.s: Since AB is parallel to CD, the co-interior angles add up to 180 degrees.
Set up the equation: 3x plus 10 plus 2x plus 30 equals 180. Simplify: 5x plus 40 equals 180.
5x equals 180 minus 40 which is 140. Divide by 5: x equals 28.

explain_questions.confirm_question: If alternate interior angles are (4y - 10) and (2y + 20), find y.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: If alternate interior angles are 4y minus 10 and 2y plus 20, find y.
nudges: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q10s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Co-interior angles sum = 180°</text></g><g id="q10s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">(3x+10)+(2x+30)=180 → 5x+40=180</text></g><g id="q10s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">5x=140 → x=28</text></g><g id="q10ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">x = 28</text></g>

practice_questions.question: Two lines AB and CD intersect at O. Angle AOC = 65 degrees. Find angle BOD.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Two lines AB and CD intersect at O. Angle AOC is 65 degrees. Find angle BOD.
steps: Angle BOD and angle AOC are vertically opposite angles.
Vertically opposite angles are always equal.
Angle BOD = 65 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Vertically opposite angles are the ones that are across the X from each other. Always equal!
practice_questions.alt: At intersection O, angles AOC and BOD are on opposite sides. They are equal = 65 degrees.

practice_questions.question: Find the angle that forms a linear pair with 135 degrees.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Find the angle that forms a linear pair with 135 degrees.
steps: Linear pair angles are supplementary.
They add up to 180 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Linear pair = straight line = 180 degrees. Just subtract from 180!
practice_questions.alt: On a straight line, angles add to 180. So the other angle = 180 - 135 = 45 degrees.

practice_questions.question: AB || CD. Corresponding angle at AB is 110 degrees. Find the corresponding angle at CD.
practice_questions.question_spoken: AB is parallel to CD. The corresponding angle at AB is 110 degrees. Find the corresponding angle at CD.
steps: AB || CD means lines are parallel.
Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.
Corresponding angle at CD = 110 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: F-shape angles (corresponding) are equal when lines are parallel. Copy the angle!
practice_questions.alt: Parallel lines make corresponding angles equal. Answer = 110 degrees.

practice_questions.question: AB || CD. Alternate interior angle at AB is 72 degrees. Find the alternate interior angle at CD.
practice_questions.question_spoken: AB is parallel to CD. The alternate interior angle at AB is 72 degrees. Find the alternate interior angle at CD.
steps: AB || CD means lines are parallel.
Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.
Alternate interior angle at CD = 72 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Z-shape angles (alternate interior) are equal when lines are parallel. Just copy the value!
practice_questions.alt: Parallel lines make alternate interior angles equal. Answer = 72 degrees.

practice_questions.question: AB || CD. One co-interior angle is 115 degrees. Find the other co-interior angle.
practice_questions.question_spoken: AB is parallel to CD. One co-interior angle is 115 degrees. Find the other co-interior angle.
steps: Co-interior angles are supplementary when lines are parallel.
They add up to 180 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: C-shape angles (co-interior) add up to 180 when lines are parallel. Subtract from 180!

practice_questions.question: Two lines intersect. One angle is 4x degrees and its vertically opposite angle is (2x + 40) degrees. Find x.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Two lines intersect. One angle is 4x degrees and its vertically opposite angle is 2x plus 40 degrees. Find x.
steps: Vertically opposite angles are equal.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Set vertically opposite angles equal to each other and solve!

practice_questions.question: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. One co-interior angle is (3x + 15) degrees and the other is (2x + 25) degrees. Find x.
practice_questions.question_spoken: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. One co-interior angle is 3x plus 15 degrees and the other is 2x plus 25 degrees. Find x.
steps: Co-interior angles sum to 180 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Co-interior angles add to 180. Add both expressions and set equal to 180, then solve!

practice_questions.question: Are the lines parallel if alternate interior angles are 78 and 78 degrees?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Are the lines parallel if alternate interior angles are 78 and 78 degrees?
steps: Check if alternate interior angles are equal.
78 degrees = 78 degrees. Yes, they are equal.
Equal alternate interior angles means lines are parallel.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Equal alternate interior angles (Z-shape) is a test for parallel lines. Equal = parallel!
practice_questions.alt: If Z-shape angles are equal, lines are parallel. 78 = 78, so yes, parallel.

practice_questions.question: Are the lines parallel if co-interior angles are 100 and 70 degrees?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Are the lines parallel if co-interior angles are 100 and 70 degrees?
steps: Check if co-interior angles add up to 180 degrees.
100 + 70 = 170 degrees. Not 180!
Wait: 100 + 70 = 170, not 180. So lines are NOT parallel.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Co-interior angles must sum to EXACTLY 180 for parallel lines. 100 + 70 = 170, not 180, so NOT parallel!
practice_questions.alt: 100 + 70 = 170. Since 170 is not 180, the lines are not parallel.

practice_questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. Find all 8 angles if one angle is 50 degrees.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. Find all 8 angles if one angle is 50 degrees.
steps: One angle = 50 degrees. Its linear pair = 180 - 50 = 130 degrees.
Vertically opposite to 50 = 50 degrees. Vertically opposite to 130 = 130 degrees.
By parallel line properties, all 4 angles at second intersection are same: 50, 130, 50, 130.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: With parallel lines, only 2 distinct angle values appear among all 8 angles. They are supplementary!
practice_questions.alt: The 8 angles are alternately 50 and 130 degrees at both intersections.

practice_questions.question: AB || CD. Angle APQ = 5x - 10 and angle PQD = 3x + 30 (alternate interior). Find angle APQ.
practice_questions.question_spoken: AB is parallel to CD. Angle APQ equals 5x minus 10 and angle PQD equals 3x plus 30. These are alternate interior angles. Find angle APQ.
steps: Alternate interior angles are equal: 5x - 10 = 3x + 30.
Angle APQ = 5(20) - 10 = 100 - 10 = 90 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Set the two alternate interior angles equal, solve for x, then substitute back!

practice_questions.question: A road crosses two parallel railway tracks. One angle formed is 55 degrees. Find all other angles at that crossing.
practice_questions.question_spoken: A road crosses two parallel railway tracks. One angle formed at the first crossing is 55 degrees. Find the other three angles at that crossing.
steps: One angle = 55 degrees. Linear pair = 180 - 55 = 125 degrees.
Vertically opposite to 55 = 55 degrees.
Vertically opposite to 125 = 125 degrees. Four angles: 55, 125, 55, 125.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: At any intersection, you only need one angle to find all four! Use linear pair and vertical angles.
practice_questions.alt: The four angles at the crossing are 55, 125, 55, 125 degrees in order.

practice_questions.question: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. The ratio of co-interior angles is 2:3. Find both angles.
practice_questions.question_spoken: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. The ratio of co-interior angles is 2:3. Find both angles.
steps: Let angles be 2k and 3k. Co-interior angles sum to 180.
Angles: 2 x 36 = 72 degrees and 3 x 36 = 108 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Ratio problem with sum known. Let angles be 2k and 3k. They sum to 180. Solve for k!

practice_questions.question: Lines l and m are parallel. A transversal makes an angle of 90 degrees with line l. What angle does it make with line m?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Lines l and m are parallel. A transversal makes an angle of 90 degrees with line l. What angle does it make with line m?
steps: The transversal makes 90 degrees with l.
Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.
The transversal also makes 90 degrees with line m. The transversal is perpendicular to both.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: A line perpendicular to one parallel line is perpendicular to ALL parallel lines!
practice_questions.alt: Corresponding angles are equal. 90 degrees at l means 90 degrees at m too.

practice_questions.question: Two streets meet at a junction forming an angle of 40 degrees. Find the three other angles at the junction.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Two streets meet at a junction forming an angle of 40 degrees. Find the three other angles formed at the junction.
steps: One angle = 40 degrees. Linear pair = 180 - 40 = 140 degrees.
Vertically opposite to 40 degrees = 40 degrees.
Vertically opposite to 140 degrees = 140 degrees.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Two lines crossing always give two pairs of equal angles. One pair = given angle, other pair = 180 minus that!
